statement: Interfaces for children must be cartoonish, brightly coloured and playful by default.
why_it_spreads: Marketing stereotypes about childhood and a one-size-fits-all view of a very broad age range.
evidence_against: NN/g research on designing for children (src-nngroup): needs differ enormously across ages and tasks; juvenile styling can patronise older children and harm usability.
Reading level, motor skill and cognitive load drive success more than cartoon aesthetics.
replacement_principle: Design for the specific developmental stage, reading level and task of the child audience, validating with that audience rather than assuming a cartoon style.
